blocks.content: **Learning Objective:** Successfully install Google Antigravity on a secure Linux VM using a 1-click interactive installation script, bypassing the need for Docker or restricted repositories.
Ensure your Cloudtop VM is provisioned and active. Open your local terminal.
2. Run the AGY Easy Install Script
We will use the unofficial AGY Easy Install manager by `wtg-codes`. This script auto-detects your environment and recommends the best method. Execute the following command in your terminal:
**Instructor Comment:** *If `curl` fails, ensure you have an active internet connection. If the `gum` UI fails to download, the script will gracefully fall back to a plain text menu.*
Once the script launches, you will see a hierarchical menu system.

items: When prompted for the installation method, select **Official Binary (.tar.gz)**.

blocks.content: **Security Note:** *While the script supports APT/DNF and Homebrew, selecting the Official Binary method is required for our corporate Cloudtop environment to bypass system repository restrictions.*

Once the script successfully completes, it will map the binaries to `~/.local/bin/antigravity`. Go back to the desktop, click on the app grid icon on the bottom left, and locate the new Antigravity icon.
